hotkey.deadFallback: The key combination could not be registered.

works.speech: Speech recognition entirely on the device (whisper.cpp), German first
works.pushToTalk: Push-to-talk with a focus-neutral recording overlay
works.injection: Text inserted straight into the application you were working in
works.typedIpc: Typed IPC surface (ts-rs bindings as the single source of truth)
works.logging: Structured logging: console, rotating JSON file, live log view

home.notYetBody: The overlay and text injection are Windows-only so far. The macOS half — a non-activating NSPanel and CGEvent insertion — is written and measured on the Mac, not blind on Windows (PLAN.md phase 1b). Further recognition languages follow as optional downloads.
home.notYetInfo: Speech recognition runs in a separate, deprivileged worker process — the process that holds the microphone and synthesises keystrokes must never be the one parsing a model file (ADR-PROJ-005).

recording.title: Push-to-talk
recording.info: Hold the combination to record, release it to insert the text into whatever application you were working in. A global shortcut is exclusive: while Huginn holds it, no other application receives it.
recording.combinationHint: Hold to speak, release to insert.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.themeHint: Huginn follows your desktop unless you tell it otherwise.
appearance.languageHint: The language of the interface. The language Huginn recognises is set with the model.

background.title: Background
background.info: Huginn is a background tool: the hotkey works from any application, but only while Huginn is running. It always keeps a tray icon — that is how you open it again, and how you quit it.
background.autostartHint: Off by default. Nothing adds itself to your startup unasked.
background.autostartFailed: The system refused to change the startup entry.
background.onCloseHint: Closing the window does not stop dictation unless you say so — the hotkey needs Huginn to be running.

speech.modelInfo: The model does the recognising — entirely on this device. The multilingual models understand German and English. A larger model handles difficult audio better but takes longer.
speech.noModel: No model is installed yet. Without one, Huginn cannot recognise anything — download one.
speech.networkNote: This download is the only connection Huginn ever opens. The server (huggingface.co) learns your IP address — nothing else. The file is verified against a checksum compiled into the program; if it does not match, it is deleted.
speech.importNote: You can load your own Whisper model file (.bin) from disk. It cannot be verified — there is no checksum compiled in for it — so it is never shown as "verified". It is still safe: model files are parsed in the deprivileged worker process, never in the one that holds the microphone and keyboard.
speech.microphoneHint: If the chosen microphone is unavailable, Huginn falls back to the default — so the sentence you are speaking is not lost.
speech.microphonesFailed: The microphones could not be listed.
speech.soundsHint: A short tone when recording starts and stops. With push-to-talk it is often the only confirmation that the microphone is really open.

commands.info: Everything you speak that is not dictation: line and paragraph breaks, optional punctuation, and macros — a spoken word that inserts a whole passage. All of it stays on the device.
commands.yourEmpty: No commands of your own yet. Add one — for example a macro word that inserts a whole passage.
commands.saveFailed: The command could not be saved.
commands.phrasesHint: One or more spoken words, separated by commas.
commands.placeholderHelp: Placeholders: {date} {time} {clipboard} {cursor} — for literal braces use {{ and }}.
commands.punctuationHint: When on, "comma" becomes ",", "period" becomes ".", and so on. In return you can no longer dictate those words literally — which is why it is off by default.
commands.builtinHint: These commands are always there. They depend on the recognition language.
commands.builtinFailed: The built-in commands could not be loaded.

help.title: Help
help.intro: {{name}} turns speech into text — straight into the application you are working in, entirely on this device.
help.privacyBody: Everything runs locally. Audio and recognised text never leave the device — no cloud, no telemetry, no stored recordings. The only outbound connection is the model download, which you start yourself.
help.how2: Speak while you hold it. An overlay shows that Huginn is listening.
help.how3: Release. The recognised text is inserted where your cursor was.
help.hotkeyBody: The combination is yours to choose under Settings → Recording. If another application already owns it, Huginn tells you at the top of the window — it will not dictate silently into nowhere.
help.commandsInfo: You set all of this under Settings → Commands.
help.commandsBody: Some things cannot be dictated, only spoken: say "new line" for a line break or "new paragraph" for a blank line. Optionally you can speak punctuation too ("comma", "period").
help.macrosBody: Macros are trigger words that insert a whole passage — for example "signature" for your sign-off. A macro's text may carry placeholders: {date}, {time}, {clipboard}, and {cursor} for where the caret should land afterwards.
help.troubleNoModel: Nothing is recognised and no model is installed? Download one under Settings → Recognition.
help.troubleHotkey: The key does nothing? Another application probably owns it — pick a different combination.
help.troubleSilent: You speak but nothing arrives? Check the microphone under Settings → Recognition, and turn the sound on to hear whether it is recording.
